cards.front: What is the primary function of the sliding window protocol?
cards.back: To efficiently transfer data between nodes by maintaining a fixed-size window of data that can be sent at a time

cards.front: What is the purpose of the maximum segment size (MSS) in sliding window protocol?
cards.back: To define the maximum size of each segment in a window, ensuring that data can be transmitted and received efficiently

cards.front: What is the sliding window protocol used for?
cards.back: For error-free data transfer between devices, providing a buffer for data transmission and reception

cards.front: Which layer in the OSI model implements the sliding window protocol?
What is the main advantage of using the sliding window protocol?
cards.back: Efficient data transfer with reduced overhead, as it allows for dynamic adjustment of the window size
